---
title: "Module Quizzes"
---

## Overview

| | |
|:--|:--|
| **Quizzes** | 6 |
| **Weight** | 10% of final grade |
| **Platform** | Blackboard |
| **Attempts** | Unlimited |

## Purpose

Module quizzes assess your understanding of key concepts from each lecture module. They are designed as a **mastery-based** learning tool --- you can retake each quiz as many times as needed to demonstrate understanding.

## Quiz Schedule

| Quiz | Module | Topics | Available After |
|:-----|:-------|:-------|:----------------|
| Quiz 1 | Foundations of Regression | Simple regression, OLS, dummy variables | Week 3 |
| Quiz 2 | Categorical Predictors | ANCOVA, one-way ANOVA, multiple comparisons | Week 5 |
| Quiz 3 | Interactions | Interaction effects, two-way ANOVA, simple slopes | Week 7 |
| Quiz 4 | Model Building & Diagnostics | Nonlinear relationships, model selection, residual diagnostics | Week 9 |
| Quiz 5 | Logistic Regression | Odds ratios, predicted probabilities, Simpson's Paradox | Week 11 |
| Quiz 6 | Logistic Diagnostics | Goodness of fit, classification, pseudo-$R^2$ | Week 12 |

## Format

- Each quiz contains multiple-choice and short-answer questions.
- Questions focus on **conceptual understanding** rather than computation.
- Quizzes are **open-book** and **open-notes**.
- Your **highest score** across all attempts is recorded.

## Grading

Quizzes are graded on a mastery scale:

| Score | Criteria |
|:-----:|:---------|
| Full credit | 80% or higher on the quiz |
| Partial credit | 60--79% on the quiz |
| Minimal credit | Below 60% on the quiz |
| No credit | Quiz not attempted |

## Tips

- Take each quiz after completing the corresponding lecture and lab material.
- Use your first attempt as a **self-assessment** to identify areas for review.
- Review the [Lecture Notes]({{< var books.lecture_notes >}}){target="_blank"} and [R Lab Book]({{< var books.r_lab_book >}}){target="_blank"} before retaking a quiz.
- There is no penalty for multiple attempts --- keep trying until you reach mastery.
